personality_voice.book_baseline: In the books, Michael Corner is a minor but textured Ravenclaw. He is direct and slightly challenging in speech (questioning Hermione's motives at the Hog's Head, immediately crediting Harry's dragon flying), curious and engaged in class (asking Slughorn about Felix Felicis), and capable of sarcasm (dismissing Luna's diadem claim with 'is lost, Luna, that's sort of the point'). He also shows frustration and confrontational directness in the Room of Requirement ('You're going to leave us in this mess?'). His voice is confident, occasionally sharp, and intellectually engaged.
personality_voice.film_portrayal: The film corpus is entirely empty. Michael Corner does not appear in any provided film scene.
personality_voice.difference: There is no film portrayal to evaluate. Every observable trait - his directness, sarcasm, intellectual curiosity, confrontational edge - is absent because the character is absent.
personality_voice.penalty_logic: A score of 0 would be appropriate for total absence, but the scoring rubric's floor for this dimension is 0. The character is not portrayed at all, so no personality or voice is preserved. A minimal score of 4 reflects that the character exists in the source material with identifiable traits, but none are carried into the films.

narrative_role_agency.book_baseline: Michael Corner serves several small but distinct narrative functions: he is a founding DA member whose presence is partly enabled by his relationship with Ginny; he participates in DA training; he acts with moral courage in DH by releasing a chained first-year under the Carrows' regime and is tortured for it - a moment that has real narrative weight (it demoralizes the DA resistance); he challenges Harry directly in the Room of Requirement; and he provides a dismissive Ravenclaw perspective on the lost diadem.
narrative_role_agency.film_portrayal: No film scenes provided. The character has no agency in the films as represented in this corpus.
narrative_role_agency.difference: All agency - his DA participation, his act of resistance and subsequent torture, his challenge to Harry, his role in the diadem conversation - is absent.
narrative_role_agency.penalty_logic: Complete absence eliminates all narrative role and agency. Score reflects near-total loss.

motivations_internal_conflict.book_baseline: Michael's motivations are modest but legible: genuine interest in learning Defence (he raises the OWL angle at the Hog's Head), romantic interest in Ginny which draws him to the DA, intellectual curiosity (Felix Felicis question), and in DH a clear moral impulse to resist the Carrows' cruelty (releasing the chained first-year at personal cost).
motivations_internal_conflict.difference: No motivations are conveyed in the films as represented in this corpus.

character_arc.book_baseline: Michael has a small but coherent arc across OotP and DH: he joins the DA partly through Ginny, trains with them, is dumped by Ginny for being a bad loser, reappears in HBP as a curious student, and by DH has become a committed enough resister to risk - and suffer - torture for a moral act. It is a flat-to-quietly-heroic arc.
character_arc.difference: The arc is entirely absent from the films as represented in this corpus.

key_relationships.book_baseline: Two relationships matter: his relationship with Ginny (romantic, ends because he is a bad loser - which characterizes both of them) and his membership in the DA cohort (peer relationship with Harry, Neville, and the Ravenclaw group). The Ginny relationship is a small but real characterization beat.
key_relationships.difference: Neither relationship is represented in the film corpus.

complexity_nuance_lost_material.book_baseline: Michael Corner's complexity is modest but real: he is not simply a background extra. He is sarcastic, intellectually engaged, romantically linked to Ginny, capable of moral courage under genuine danger, and willing to challenge Harry directly. The DH torture detail is the single most humanizing and dramatically significant moment - it gives him weight beyond his minor status.
complexity_nuance_lost_material.difference: All nuance, including the most significant moment (the torture for releasing a chained student), is absent.
complexity_nuance_lost_material.penalty_logic: Complete absence of all complexity. The most dramatically significant book material - his act of resistance and its consequences - is entirely lost. Minimal score assigned.
